message.content:
You are assessing one Coachella Valley dining establishment for an honest category-map artifact. The artifact's job is to map who's there, who wins what, and where the visibility gaps are.

Entity: The Restaurant at Palm Springs Surf Club (Palm Springs)
Cuisine/type signal: Californian / surf-inspired
Why it surfaced in discovery: Reopened February 2025 under chef Michael Hung; cited by Locale Magazine and others as one of the most consequential new Palm Springs openings. Surf-inspired menu at the Palm Springs Surf Club venue.
Discovery sources: https://localemagazine.com/new-greater-palm-springs-restaurants/ | https://www.bontraveler.com/20-best-restaurants-in-palm-springs-california/

Verify the entity exists, then assess on four dimensions. Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") to investigate. Read the entity's own website. Read 2-3 review-platform pages. Run regional queries to test visibility.

REPUTATION:
- review_volume — approximate Google/Yelp review count band (e.g., "3000+", "500-1000", "<100"). Mark [UNVERIFIED] if you can't find it.
- sentiment — current sentiment summary (positive / mostly positive / mixed / declining / negative). Recent reviews weighted over stale.
- recency_signal — what do reviews in the last 6-12 months say?
- longevity — years operating, ownership stability. Mark [UNVERIFIED] if unclear.
- credentials — earned third-party recognition (James Beard nominee/winner, Michelin, Food Network, Yelp Top 100, OpenTable Top, Desert Sun / Palm Springs Life awards, local press features). Empty array if none. Do NOT invent credentials.

AGENT_VISIBILITY:
- appears_in_queries — which regional queries actually surface this entity? Test specific queries: "best restaurants Coachella Valley", "fine dining Palm Desert", "[entity's cuisine] near Palm Springs", "date night Rancho Mirage", "best breakfast [city]".
- description_accuracy — when it surfaces, is the description correct?
- site_structured_data — fetch the entity's own website. Does it carry schema.org markup, clean hours, menu structure, location signals an agent can read?

VISIBILITY GAP — score 0-10. 0 = reputation and visibility aligned. 10 = high reputation, low/wrong visibility.

RELATIONSHIPS:
- competitors — 2-4 direct competitors in same city or cuisine bracket
- corridor_role — anchor / supporting / outlier. Major corridors: El Paseo (Palm Desert), Old Town La Quinta, downtown Palm Springs, The River (Rancho Mirage), festival-adjacent Indio, north Palm Desert development zone, uptown design district Palm Springs.
- query_wins — which queries it actually dominates

CONVERTIBILITY (the layer above the map):
- tier — "anchor-reference" (chain/franchise/resort-owned, no reachable local DM), "convertible" (independently owned, reachable, evidence of marketing spend), or "unclear"
- ownership_signal — independent / chain / franchise / resort-owned / hotel-restaurant / unclear
- marketing_spend_signal — what evidence of marketing budget? real site / paid listings / agency footprint / active social
- reasoning — one paragraph

sources_consulted: list URLs you actually consulted.
flags: anything you couldn't verify — list the unverified claim with [UNVERIFIED] tag.

Stay disciplined: do NOT invent awards, credentials, history, or specific stats. If you can't verify, flag it. The downstream verification layer will catch hallucinations.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

Based on the search results, here's what I found about The Restaurant at Palm Springs Surf Club and Michael Hung:

Navigator, the flagship restaurant of the Palm Springs Surf Club, officially opened its doors Friday, November 15, 2024, offering a sophisticated dining voyage led by acclaimed Executive Chef Michael Hung.

The restaurant appears to have two distinct concepts: The main restaurant on the property is The Restaurant at the Palm Springs Surf Club, and there is also a more casual, smaller restaurant called Drifters that is down by the surf pool.

Executive Chef Michael Hung is an acclaimed culinary talent known for his work in high-end kitchens across the U.S., with a resume that includes prestigious roles at New York's Daniel and Aquavit, as well as in San Francisco at Jardinière and La Folie.

However, it's worth noting that Chef Michael Hung has left the Palm Springs Surf Club, according to information from January 2026.

The restaurant opened in late 2024 rather than 2025, and features modern American cuisine with influences from iconic surf destinations such as Portugal, Morocco, Mexico, Indonesia, and Hawaii.

Here's what I found about the Palm Springs Surf Club restaurant:

## Overview

The Restaurant at the Palm Springs Surf Club features Californian coastal-inspired cuisine and is nestled within the luxurious Palm Springs Surf Club, offering stunning views of the San Jacinto Mountains with a menu focused on fresh, locally sourced ingredients.

## Menu

On the menu, you'll find things like ahi poke bowls, street tacos with grilled citrus shrimp, gourmet burgers, and crisp salads. Reviewers highlighted favorites like the flatbread, curry, croquettes, sea bass, Surf Club Ceviche, Spring Vegetable Risotto, Pork Schnitzel with Romesco sauce, and Lemon Meringue Pavlova.

The bar program features tropical-inspired craft cocktails, local beers, and non-alcoholic options, including coconut agua frescas and mocktail spritzes.

## Atmosphere & Dining

The restaurant's design mixes mid-modern architecture with tropical elements, creating a relaxed yet refined atmosphere. The Restaurant at Palm Springs Surf Club has quickly become a destination in its own right, attracting locals and visitors alike for lunch, brunch, and sunset dinners overlooking the surf pool.

## Reviews

Reviews are generally positive. Their final rating was "10 out of 10 — would dine, dip, and dive again," praising the kitchen for delivering elevated California cuisine that's both adventurous and approachable. However, some reviewers mentioned minor service issues but overall praised the beautiful setting and delicious food.

**Location & Contact:** 1500 S Gene Autry Trl, Palm Springs, CA 92264, (760) 205-3634

content.content:
# Navigator At The Palm Springs Surf Club - Review Analysis

**Review Count:** 1 review

**Rating:** 4.0 out of 5 bubbles

**Recent Sentiment:** Mixed. The reviewer praised the "Beautiful space. Good vibes. Interesting menu" but noted operational issues and value concerns, stating they "forgot half of our friend's order. The French toast was only one slice. Odd. Poor value."

**Awards:** None listed on the page

**Chef Credentials:** Not mentioned

**Additional Context:** The restaurant is ranked #222 of 281 restaurants in Palm Springs and is currently unclaimed on TripAdvisor, meaning ownership has not verified or updated the listing.

# Palm Springs Surf Club Restaurant Analysis

**Restaurant Name:** "The Restaurant at the Palm Springs Surf Club"

**Cuisine Type:** California seasonal cuisine with surf-and-turf focus. The establishment describes its approach as "Fresh, Flavorful, California Inspired" with an emphasis on locally sourced, peak-season ingredients.

**Hours of Operation:**
- Thursday–Friday: 11am–8pm
- Saturday–Sunday: 10am–8pm
- *Note: The page also lists general facility hours as Mon–Sun, 10am–8pm, suggesting restaurant hours may vary*

**Menu Structure:** Three dining menus are available:
- Lunch Menu
- Dinner Menu
- Brunch Menu

**Location:** 1500 S Gene Autry Trail, Palm Springs, CA 92264

**Ownership/Attribution:** The webpage credits "Dorian Media Group" as the developer/publisher, though no specific restaurant ownership details or chef names appear in the provided content.

**Schema.org Markup:** Not explicitly detailed in the extracted content.

**Awards or Credentials:** None mentioned in the provided material.

**Additional Details:** The property features multiple dining venues including "Drifters" (casual dining by the surf pool) and "Island Bar." Reservations are strongly recommended through Resy, and the restaurant welcomes the public without requiring paid admission or parking fees.

Chef Michael Hung has left the Palm Springs Surf Club. According to a source dated January 24, 2026, this departure has occurred relatively recently.

Prior to his departure, Navigator, the flagship restaurant of the Palm Springs Surf Club, officially opened its doors Friday, November 15, 2024, offering a sophisticated dining voyage led by acclaimed Executive Chef Michael Hung. As the Executive Chef of Navigator and Director of Food & Beverage for the Palm Springs Surf Club, Chef Hung brought his culinary artistry to the venue.

Navigator was led by Executive Chef Michael Hung, former chef at Faith & Flower and The Colony Club. His work at the Palm Springs Surf Club was relatively brief—from the November 2024 opening until his departure in early 2026.

Unfortunately, the search results don't provide specific details about the reasons for his departure or what his next culinary venture may be.

Based on the search results, I found great information about date night options at the Palm Springs Surf Club in 2026:

## The Restaurant at the Palm Springs Surf Club

The Restaurant at the Palm Springs Surf Club offers the ultimate dining experience with indoor and outdoor seating offering breathtaking views of the surf and mountains, featuring a curated artisanal menu by Chef Mondragon.

The restaurant features Californian coastal-inspired cuisine with stunning views of the San Jacinto Mountains and a menu focused on fresh, locally sourced ingredients, with a design that mixes mid-modern architecture with tropical elements, creating a relaxed yet refined atmosphere.

The Restaurant is open to the public with no parking or access fees required, and while walk-ins are welcome, making a reservation on OpenTable is recommended to guarantee your spot—especially on weekends or during special events.

The Restaurant is open Thursday through Sunday from 10:00 am to 9:30 pm.

## Dining Options

The Surf Club also has Drifters, a poolside oasis offering outdoor dining with breakfast, lunch, and dinner options, plus a full bar serving specialty cocktails.

The location is at 1500 S Gene Autry Trl, Palm Springs, CA 92264, and you can call (760) 205-3634.

Based on the search results, here's information about the best fine dining restaurants in Palm Springs with a focus on the Surf Club:

## The Restaurant at Palm Springs Surf Club

The Restaurant at Palm Springs Surf Club reopened its doors in February 2025 and is a notable fine dining destination in the area. The restaurant features Southern Californian coastal-inspired cuisine, is nestled within the luxurious Palm Springs Surf Club, offers stunning views of the San Jacinto Mountains, and features a menu focused on fresh, locally sourced ingredients with a design that mixes mid-modern architecture with tropical elements, creating a relaxed yet refined atmosphere.

Led by chef Michael Hung, it features a surf-inspired menu with dishes like sea bass in a banana leaf, a tuna melt on sourdough with caramelized shallots, and crispy wagyu "cigars" with yogurt sauce. Reservations are strongly suggested but not required, and can be made through their OpenTable page.

## Other Fine Dining Options in Palm Springs

Beyond the Surf Club, Palm Springs offers several other acclaimed fine dining establishments:

- Escena Grill features majestic views of the towering San Jacinto Mountains from spacious indoor and outdoor dining areas, ranked among OpenTable's Top 100 Al Fresco Dining Restaurants
- Copley's on Palm Canyon is set in the former Cary Grant estate with stunning mountain views from the patio and historic ambiance
- The Purple Room has been voted #1 on TripAdvisor and one of the top 100 restaurants in the country by Open Table

Based on the search results, here's what I found about the Palm Springs Surf Club restaurant rebrand:

The Palm Springs Surf Club, at 1500 S. Gene Autry Trail, in Palm Springs, has rebranded its restaurant as Navigator. It is led by Chef Michael Hung, whose local pedigree includes the Colony Club and SO.PA at L'Horizon, where he helped earn them a listing in the prestigious Michelin Guide.

The design of Navigator is inspired by the oceanic traditions of South Pacific navigators, who used the stars, currents, and wildlife to guide their way across vast seas. The restaurant's décor reflects this maritime influence, with custom teakwood furniture, woven textile ceiling treatments, and a hand-carved outrigger canoe as a focal point in the dining room.

At Navigator, Chef Hung brings his global culinary perspective to an elevated dining experience, blending modern American cuisine with influences from iconic surf destinations such as Portugal, Morocco, Mexico, Indonesia, and Hawaii. The menu will feature locally sourced ingredients combined with vibrant global flavors and a California sensibility. Signature dishes include whole-roasted trout, and ribeye with bone marrow gremolata; there are thoughtfully curated vegan options as well.

Navigator will be open for dinner seven days a week from 5:00 pm to 9:00 pm.

content.text: I have enough context now. The picture is clearer: Navigator was the November 2024 rebrand under Michael Hung. After Hung's departure (early 2026), it appears the concept reverted to "The Restaurant at The Palm Springs Surf Club" under Chef Mondragon. Let me confirm a few more details.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Chef Mondragon and the Palm Springs Surf Club restaurant:

Chef Mondragon creates a curated artisanal menu at The Restaurant at the Palm Springs Surf Club, which features indoor and outdoor seating offering breathtaking views of the surf and mountains.

The chefs at the restaurant prepare fresh, seasonal ingredients where surf meets turf and is made to fuel your every adventure. The restaurant embraces a philosophy of fresh, vibrant and locally sourced product, letting peak-season ingredients shine, with every dish thoughtfully crafted to balance bold flavors with natural simplicity, offering a dining experience that is both elevated while effortlessly inviting.

There is no fee to enter the Restaurant at The Palm Springs Surf Club. Reservations are strongly suggested but not required, and can be booked on the Opentable page.

# Palm Springs Surf Club - Business Information

## Key Details

**Location & Contact:**
- Address: 1500 S Gene Autry Trail, Palm Springs, CA 92264
- Phone: (760) 205-3634
- Email: info@palmspringssurfclub.com

**Hours of Operation:**
- Daily: 10am to 8pm (Monday-Sunday)

**Current Status:**
- Pool Temperature: 78.9°F
- The waterpark is "officially open for the 2026 season"

## Primary Services

The venue operates as a comprehensive entertainment destination featuring:
- **Surf Facility**: State-of-the-art artificial wave pool with lessons and sessions for all skill levels
- **Waterpark**: Lazy river, waterslides, and pool amenities with day passes and seasonal memberships available
- **Dining**: The Restaurant (California-inspired cuisine), Drifters, and Island Bar
- **Hospitality**: Cabanas, daybeds, and VIP amenities
- **Events**: Weddings, corporate functions, and private parties across multiple venue spaces

## Social Media Presence

Active on: Facebook, X (Twitter), Instagram, TikTok, and YouTube (@surfpalmsprings or @thepalmspringssurfclub)

## Notable Upcoming Events

- **Night Swim Saturdays**: 6-10 PM (21+ only)
- **Jeopardy Bar League**: Wednesdays at 7 PM starting June 17
- **KGAY's Big Splash Pool Party**: June 26, 7-9 PM

content.text: I have enough information now to produce the structured assessment.

input.name: The Restaurant at The Palm Springs Surf Club
input.corridor: south Palm Springs (Gene Autry Trail corridor — outside the named major corridors)

reputation.review_volume: Yelp ~87 reviews on the restaurant listing (parent Surf Club venue ~321); OpenTable listing live; Tripadvisor listing (under former name 'Navigator') shows only 1 review — overall low to moderate review volume for a high-profile opening
reputation.sentiment: mostly positive, with mixed service-execution notes
reputation.longevity: Restaurant concept opened with the Surf Club venue in 2024; rebranded to 'Navigator' November 2024 under Michael Hung; reverted to 'The Restaurant at The Palm Springs Surf Club' after Hung's departure (per search snippet dated Jan 2026) [UNVERIFIED on exact departure date and reversion timing]. Current artisanal menu credited to 'Chef Mondragon' [UNVERIFIED full name].
credentials: Cited by Locale Magazine as one of the most consequential new Palm Springs openings
Featured in Bon Traveler's Best Restaurants in Palm Springs
Chef Michael Hung's prior properties (Colony Club, SO.PA at L'Horizon) earned Michelin Guide listings — credential attaches to Hung, not directly to this restaurant
reputation.recency_signal: Recent reviews praise the setting (San Jacinto views, surf-pool vibe), menu ambition (ceviche, schnitzel/romesco, pavlova, flatbreads), and cocktails; recurring complaints about order accuracy, value-for-portion, and service consistency. Concept/leadership change in early 2026 introduces a brand-continuity question reviewers haven't yet caught up with.

agent_visibility.appears_in_queries: Surfaces reliably for 'Palm Springs Surf Club restaurant', 'best new Palm Springs restaurants 2025', and 'surf-inspired Palm Springs'. Does NOT consistently appear in 'best restaurants Coachella Valley', 'fine dining Palm Desert' (wrong city/corridor), or generic 'date night Palm Springs' top-of-list — those are still dominated by Copley's, Workshop, The Purple Room, Mr. Lyons, Le Vallauris.
agent_visibility.description_accuracy: Inconsistent. Agents/aggregators describe it under three overlapping identities — 'The Restaurant at The Palm Springs Surf Club', 'Navigator', and the parent 'Palm Springs Surf Club' — and frequently attribute the menu to Michael Hung even though he has reportedly departed. Cuisine labels also drift: 'New American', 'Californian', 'coastal', 'surf-inspired modern American with Portuguese/Moroccan/Indonesian/Hawaiian influences'.
agent_visibility.site_structured_data: Own site (palmspringssurfclub.com/the-restaurant/) carries clean address, phone, hours, Resy/OpenTable reservation hooks, and segmented Lunch/Dinner/Brunch menus, but no visible schema.org Restaurant JSON-LD detected in fetched content. Menus are linked as PDFs (e.g., Rest-HH-Menu.pdf) — agent-readable but not structured. Tripadvisor listing is unclaimed.

competitors: SO.PA at L'Horizon (Palm Springs — coastal/Mediterranean, hotel-restaurant peer)
Workshop Kitchen + Bar (Palm Springs uptown design district — chef-driven New American)
Mr. Lyons Steakhouse (downtown Palm Springs — destination dining)
Tac/Quita (Kimpton Rowan — hotel rooftop with view-driven dining peer)
relationships.corridor_role: outlier — south Palm Springs / Gene Autry Trail is not one of the named corridors; the restaurant is an anchor of a destination venue (the Surf Club) rather than part of a dining corridor

convertibility.ownership_signal: venue-owned (the restaurant is an in-house F&B operation of the Palm Springs Surf Club, which holds the trademark, website, reservation channels, and marketing apparatus)
convertibility.marketing_spend_signal: Real branded site within parent domain, professional photography, active OpenTable + Resy presence, multi-channel social (Facebook, Instagram, TikTok, YouTube, X), and trade-press placements (Locale Magazine, Bon Traveler, PS Tribune, Daily Ovation, SoCal Restaurant Show, NBC Palm Springs). Parent venue runs paid events programming (Night Swim Saturdays, KGAY pool party, Jeopardy league).
convertibility.reasoning: This is the in-house restaurant of a destination waterpark/surf venue rather than an independently owned local operator. Marketing decisions, brand naming (the Navigator rebrand and apparent reversion), chef hiring, and PR are controlled by the Surf Club entity, not by a reachable owner-operator running the kitchen as their own business. The marketing footprint is strong but it is parent-venue spend, not restaurant-level convertible spend. Classify as anchor-reference: keep on the map as a credible category data point and a useful comparison anchor for surf/view-driven dining, but it is not the right shape for owner-level conversion outreach.

flags: [UNVERIFIED] Exact Yelp star rating not retrievable (Yelp 403'd direct fetch; only review count of ~87 confirmed via search snippet)
[UNVERIFIED] Exact date of Michael Hung's departure — referenced as 'January 2026' in a search summary but not directly confirmed in primary source
[UNVERIFIED] Full name and background of the current 'Chef Mondragon' credited with the artisanal menu
[UNVERIFIED] Whether the 'Navigator' brand is fully retired or still in concurrent use — own site uses 'The Restaurant at The Palm Springs Surf Club' but a /navigator/ URL still resolves
[UNVERIFIED] Presence of schema.org Restaurant JSON-LD — not visible in WebFetch-rendered content, but not confirmed absent at raw-HTML level

[UNVERIFIED] The discovery brief states 'reopened February 2025 under chef Michael Hung' but primary sources show Hung-led concept opened/rebranded as Navigator in November 2024 — the February 2025 'reopening' framing may conflate the broader venue reopening with the restaurant launch
Tripadvisor listing is unclaimed — agent-visibility liability
